0:{"rsc":["$","$1","c",{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"page-enter","children":[["$","$L2",null,{}],["$","article",null,{"className":"article-detail","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"container-tight","children":[["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog","className":"back-link","children":"← Back to all essays"}],["$","header",null,{"className":"article-header","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"article-header-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"children":"03"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Web"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"January 30, 2026"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["5 min"," read"]}]]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"The Edge Is Just a Computer (a Smaller One)"}],["$","p",null,{"style":{"fontSize":19,"color":"var(--ink-soft)","lineHeight":1.5,"margin":0,"maxWidth":600},"children":"Edge functions feel magical until they don't. A practical mental model for what actually changes when your code runs close to the user."}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"container-tight","children":["$","div",null,{"className":"img-slot hero-image","data-label":"Web","aria-hidden":true}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"container-tight","children":["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","p","0",{"className":"lede","children":"'Run it at the edge' gets thrown around like it's free. It isn't — it's a trade. Once you see the trade clearly, the magic becomes a tool."}],["$","h2","1",{"children":"What you gain"}],["$","p","2",{"children":"Latency to your code drops because the code is near the user. For anything that can be computed without a round trip to your primary database, this is a real and large win."}],["$","h2","3",{"children":"What you pay"}],["$","ul","4",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"children":"Distance to your data. The edge is close to the user and far from your database. A chatty handler can be slower at the edge than in one region."}],["$","li","1",{"children":"A smaller runtime. No full Node API, tight CPU and memory budgets, cold paths you can't always see."}],["$","li","2",{"children":"Harder debugging. 'It works on my machine' is now 'it works in one of forty regions.'"}]]}],["$","div","5",{"className":"pull","children":["“","Put computation at the edge. Keep state behind it. Cache the boundary between them.","”"]}],["$","p","6",{"children":"Auth checks, redirects, A/B assignment, personalization headers — perfect for the edge. Multi-step transactional writes — keep them in a region next to your data and let the edge just route there."}],["$","p","7",{"children":"The edge isn't a different paradigm. It's the same computer, smaller, in more places. Design for that and it stops surprising you."}]]}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"container","style":{"marginTop":96,"paddingTop":48,"borderTop":"1px solid var(--rule-soft)"},"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"eyebrow","style":{"marginBottom":16},"children":"Read next"}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog/shipping-fast-without-breaking-things","className":"article-row","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"article-num","children":"01"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"article-title","children":"Shipping Fast Without Breaking Things"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"article-excerpt","children":"Speed and stability aren't opposites. A look at the small habits that let small teams deploy many times a day with confidence."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"article-meta","children":[["$","div",null,{"children":"April 28, 2026"}],["$","div",null,{"style":{"marginTop":4},"children":"Engineering"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"article-read","children":["read",["$","br",null,{}],"6 min"]}]]}]]}]]}]]}],[["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/myblog/_next/static/chunks/0ij507p0cynli.js","async":true}]],["$","$L4",null,{"children":["$","$5",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@6"}]}]]}],"isPartial":false,"staleTime":300,"varyParams":null,"buildId":"cq9Q9J7UkxT6Kp5YPUbwV"}
